Instructions for applying a patch to the ESP8266 SDK to adjust memory allocation for JerryScript.

# Purpose
The content describes a patch to the ESP8266 SDK linker script to optimize memory allocation for JerryScript code. The patch modifies the linker script `eagle.app.v6.common.ld` to ensure that JerryScript code is placed in the `irom` section instead of the smaller `iram` section, which cannot accommodate all the code. This is achieved by changing the order of sections and explicitly directing the linker to place specific JerryScript libraries in the `irom0_0_seg`. Additionally, the patch updates the `eagle.app.v6.ld` file to increase the size of the `irom0_0_seg` memory segment, allowing it to hold more code and data. The instructions also include steps to extract `setjmp` and `longjmp` functions from the SDK, which are necessary for the operation of JerryScript on the ESP8266 platform.
